_protocol: Calibration topic — prospective election forecast. Populated 2026-04-11. Update on each new generic ballot release, special election result, or major political development.

meta.title: US Midterms 2026 — House Control
meta.question: Which party will control the US House of Representatives after the November 2026 midterm elections?
meta.resolution: AP/Decision Desk calls House majority on or after Election Night, November 3, 2026. Controlling party must hold 218+ seats. Final seat counts certified by mid-December 2026.

tagConfig._docs: directionHints: positive = evidence favors this H, negative = evidence cuts against. POLL data is weak signal. EVENT (special election result, candidate filing) is strong.

_priorJustification: BASE RATE: Since 1946, the president's party loses House seats in 17 of 19 midterm cycles (~89%). Average loss ~25 seats. GOP currently holds ~220 seats, needing to lose only ~3 net to lose majority.

STRUCTURAL: Trump approval ~41% overall, 31% on economy (April 2026 CNN). Historical regression (Abramowitz 'Time for Change' model) maps presidential approval at 40-45% to ~20-30 seat losses for the president's party.

GENERIC BALLOT: Dems leading by approximately +6-8pp in April 2026. A generic ballot lead of +5pp or more in April of a midterm year has historically corresponded to a House flip in 4 of 5 comparable cycles.

COUNTERWEIGHT — WAR RALLY: Active US military engagement (Iran/Hormuz) may generate temporary rally-round-the-flag effect, compressing Democratic advantage. Historical war rallies average 8-12pp in approval, but decay within 3-6 months.

COUNTERWEIGHT — UNUSUAL DIVERGENCE: Generic ballot polling showing GOP performing better than Trump individual approval would predict. This divergence (noted April 2026) is atypical and introduces model uncertainty.
COUNTERWEIGHT — REDISTRICTING/INCUMBENCY: GOP incumbency advantage in drawn districts slightly mutes national swing translation.

NET ASSESSMENT: Strong historical prior for H1 (Dem flip), moderated by war context and polling divergence. H2 (Dems gain/fall short) is the second most probable — captures scenarios where Dem enthusiasm is high but geographic efficiency losses and redistricting hold the line.
PRIOR SOURCES: Abramowitz (2018), Silver/538 historical models, Cook Political Report seat-count methodology.

H1.label: Democrats flip House (218+ seats)
H1.description: Democrats net enough gains to exceed 218 seats. Requires approximately a net shift of ~3 seats from current GOP margin. Historical base rate + current approval environment make this the modal outcome. A generic ballot advantage of +5pp or more, sustained through October, is the key threshold.

H2.label: Democrats gain seats but fall short of majority
H2.description: Democrats make meaningful gains (5-15 seats) but GOP retains majority below 218D seats. Consistent with a generic ballot lead of +2 to +4pp being partially absorbed by geographic/districting inefficiency. More likely if war rally effect persists into fall or Dem candidate recruitment underperforms.

H3.label: Republicans hold — status quo (+/- 5 seats from current margin)
H3.description: Election outcome roughly matches current seat distribution. Would represent a historic deviation from midterm pattern — only the 2002 cycle (post-9/11) and 1998 cycle (Clinton impeachment backlash) saw the president's party hold or gain. Requires sustained war rally effect, generic ballot narrowing to near-parity, or major Democratic collapse (scandal, candidate quality, GOTV failure).

H4.label: Republicans gain seats
H4.description: GOP nets additional seats over current ~220. Historically very rare for the president's party at this approval level. Would require a reversal of every current trend: generic ballot, approval, economic sentiment. Possible only under a major geopolitical/economic shock that completely reframes the election as a wartime mandate vote. Tail risk only.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. Justified by historical base rate (89% opp gains midterm), Trump approval ~41%/31% economy, generic ballot Dems +6-8pp. Moderated by active war rally effect and unusual generic ballot/presidential approval divergence.

genericBallotTracker.label: Generic Congressional Ballot — Democratic Advantage
genericBallotTracker.description: The aggregate generic congressional ballot (D vs R). Key threshold: +5pp or more sustained through September correlates strongly with House flip. Values below +3pp suggest GOP hold territory. Track RealClearPolitics average and FiveThirtyEight tracker.

presidentialApproval.label: Trump Approval — Overall and Economy
presidentialApproval.description: Presidential approval is the strongest single structural predictor of midterm outcomes. Sub-45% overall is historically associated with significant seat losses. Economy-specific approval below 35% amplifies the signal. Track monthly.

specialElectionTracker.label: Special Election Results — Democratic Overperformance vs. Baseline
specialElectionTracker.description: Special elections held in competitive districts before November are the best real-time signal of national environment shift. Track Democratic margin vs. 2024 presidential baseline in same district. Consistent overperformance of +5pp or more historically predicts House flips.

candidateRecruitment.label: Candidate Filing Quality — Competitive Districts
candidateRecruitment.value: Filing deadline varies by state; most May-July 2026
candidateRecruitment.description: Strong Dem recruitment (recruiting proven candidates in top 30 target seats) is a leading indicator 6 months out. GOP holds approximately 18-22 seats in districts Biden won or nearly won in 2024. Those filing outcomes are critical. Monitor DCCC Red-to-Blue list and NRCC Patriot program.

fundraisingDifferential.label: DCCC vs NRCC Fundraising Differential
fundraisingDifferential.description: Cash-on-hand differential between the two party campaign committees. DCCC advantage of $20M+ has historically correlated with strong recruitment and advertising dominance in competitive districts. Reported quarterly with FEC.

warRallyDecay.label: Iran War Rally Effect — Approval Delta
warRallyDecay.value: Estimated +5 to +8pp rally (April 2026), expected to decay by Q3
warRallyDecay.description: Estimates the remaining approval boost attributable to the active Iran/Hormuz military engagement. Historical rally effects (Kuwait 1990, Afghanistan 2001, Iraq 2003) peak within 30-60 days and decay to baseline within 3-6 months. If Hormuz conflict escalates/expands, rally could persist longer. If it stabilizes or ends, disappears. This directly affects H3/H4 probability.

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — polling averages, filed FEC reports, certified election results. Not pundit commentary.
Tier 1 = near-resolution-quality signal. Tier 2 = strong leading indicator. Tier 3 = suggestive/confirming.
Anti-indicators: observable events that would reduce each hypothesis's probability.

tier1_critical.desc: Democrat wins a special House election in a district Trump carried by 5+ points in 2024. Observable: certified special election result.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H1 +12pp, H2 +5pp, H3 -10pp, H4 -7pp. Historical: Dem flips in hostile territory (e.g., PA-18 2018, KS-04 2017) preceded wave elections.

tier1_critical.desc: Generic ballot average (RCP or 538) reaches and holds Dems +5pp or better for 30+ consecutive days entering October 2026. Observable: published polling averages.
Trump overall approval falls below 38% in two or more major polls within the same 2-week window. Observable: published polling crosstabs from ABC/WaPo, CNN, Gallup, or equivalent.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H1 +10pp, H3 -7pp, H4 -5pp. Sub-40% approval historically maps to 25+ seat losses.

tier1_critical.desc: Cook Political Report, Sabato Crystal Ball, AND Inside Elections all simultaneously rate Dems as favored to win House majority (not merely competitive). Observable: published ratings change.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H1 +20pp, H2 -5pp, H3 -10pp, H4 -5pp. These models are lagging but consensus shift is high-confidence signal.

tier2_strong.desc: DCCC announces credentialed (prior officeholder, military veteran, or high-profile recruits) candidates in 25+ of their top 30 target seats by August 1, 2026. Observable: DCCC candidate announcements, FEC filings.
DCCC outraises NRCC by $15M+ in any single FEC quarterly report (Q2, Q3, or Q4 2026). Observable: FEC quarterly filings.
Generic ballot shows Dems +3pp or better for all of Q3 2026 (July-September). Observable: RCP average monthly checkpoints.
Trump economy approval stays below 35% in September 2026 (one month pre-election) in at least 2 major polls. Observable: published crosstabs.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +5pp, H2 +2pp, H3 -5pp, H4 -2pp. Economy approval is the strongest within-cycle predictor.

tier2_strong.desc: Democrat outperforms 2024 presidential baseline by +5pp or more in any special or off-cycle election (including state-level) held before October 2026. Observable: certified results plus 2024 Biden baseline in same geography.
Trump approval returns to pre-Hormuz baseline (approximately 39-41% range) in at least 3 consecutive polls, confirming rally effect dissipation. Observable: monthly tracking polls.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +5pp, H3 -3pp, H4 -2pp. Removes structural support for H3/H4.

tier2_strong.desc: 5 or more Republican incumbents in Biden-won or competitive (PVI R+5 or less) districts announce they will not seek re-election. Observable: public retirement announcements by filing deadlines.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +6pp, H2 +2pp, H3 -5pp, H4 -3pp. Open seats are significantly easier to flip than incumbent-held.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Trump approval stays below 42% in every major poll for 60+ consecutive days. Observable: weekly/biweekly tracking data.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 +2pp, H3 -1pp, H4 -1pp. Confirms current trajectory, modest update.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Abortion rights ballot measures qualify for November ballot in 3+ competitive states with competitive House seats (e.g., AZ, NC, PA, MI). Observable: state certification of ballot measures.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 +3pp, H2 +1pp, H3 -3pp, H4 -1pp. Turnout mobilizer for Dem-leaning voters, per 2022/2024 pattern.

tier3_suggestive.desc: BLS or BEA releases data showing measurable consumer impact of Section 122 tariffs (CPI spike 0.3pp+ month-over-month or retail sales decline) attributed to tariffs in news coverage. Observable: BLS monthly CPI release, major media attribution.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 +2pp, H2 +1pp, H3 -2pp, H4 -1pp. Concrete economic pain translates to ballot box.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Generic ballot enthusiasm (likely voter screen vs. registered voter screen) narrows to less than 3pp difference in October 2026 polls. Observable: poll crosstabs with RV/LV screens.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 +2pp, H3 -2pp. Dem enthusiasm historically lags in midterms; closing the gap is a necessary condition for translation of generic lead to seats.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Cook Political Report expands list of 'Toss Up' or 'Lean Dem' seats beyond 25 by September 2026. Observable: Cook monthly ratings updates.

Major domestic protest movement against Iran war emerges (100K+ participants in a single event, OR sustained weekly protests in 10+ cities) OR Congress holds contentious war powers vote that breaks party lines. Observable: news coverage, Capitol vote records.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 +3pp, H3 -2pp, H4 -1pp. War protest historically mobilizes opposition base.

anti_indicators.desc: Generic ballot average narrows to Dems +1pp or less (or Republicans lead) in two consecutive monthly averages before October 2026. Observable: RCP average.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H1 -15pp, H2 -5pp. At generic ballot parity, historical models show GOP holds majority with districting advantage.

anti_indicators.desc: Republican outperforms 2024 presidential baseline by +3pp or more in a special election held in a competitive or swing district. Observable: certified results.
Generic ballot reaches and holds Dems +6pp or more through October. At this level, historical models predict a full flip, making H2 (gains/fall short) less likely. Observable: RCP average.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H2 -8pp (probability mass moves to H1). H2 is squeezed from above when the environment is strong enough for a flip.

anti_indicators.desc: If Trump approval remains below 42% through Q3 2026 without recovery, status-quo hold becomes historically implausible. Observable: monthly tracking.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H3 -8pp, H4 -5pp. Historical GOP hold cycles (2002) required approval 60%+; 2002 Bush had 63% approval.

anti_indicators.desc: Trump economy approval fails to recover above 40% by September 2026. Observable: September 2026 economy approval in major polls.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H4 -8pp. Republican seat gains at sub-40% economic approval are historically unprecedented in the modern era.

anti_indicators.desc: No major Democratic Party-wide scandal or national candidate quality collapse materializes by October. Observable: absence of event. Fire this as 'CONFIRMED_ABSENT' if October passes with no such event.
anti_indicators.note: This is a null-event indicator — fire it if the event definitively does NOT occur by October 15, 2026.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H4 -5pp, H3 -3pp. H4 essentially requires a Dem self-immolation event.

actorModel.description: Election outcomes are driven by structural environment (approval, economy, war) and by specific actor decisions that shift recruitment, enthusiasm, and turnout. Five primary actors.

trump.name: Donald Trump
trump.role: President. His approval rating IS the single largest structural predictor of this election outcome. His policy decisions (tariffs, Iran war management, domestic agenda) drive the environment.
trump.decisionStyle: Impulsive, media-maximizing, loyalty-testing. Escalates when cornered. Will dominate news cycle regardless of electoral calculus.
biases: Believes electoral invincibility — may continue maximalist policies even as they poll badly
Prone to endorsing weak candidates who pass loyalty test but lose general elections
Rally-obsessed: gauges support by crowd size, not polling averages
filters: Congressional Republicans facing tough races may publicly distance from specific policies without breaking from Trump personally
War commander role provides some insulation from domestic economic criticism
overrides: If Iran war escalates dramatically (US KIA spike, draft talk, quagmire narrative), president loses control of rally framing
If tariff recession hits visibly by September, approval floor could crack below 38%

democratic_leadership.name: Democratic Congressional Leadership (Jeffries/Schumer)
democratic_leadership.role: Responsible for recruitment, messaging, and resource allocation to competitive districts. Their decisions determine whether Dem structural advantage translates to seats.
democratic_leadership.decisionStyle: Institutionalist, data-driven, risk-averse. Will prioritize proven candidates over progressive insurgents in competitive districts.
biases: May over-invest in expensive media markets (CA, NY) rather than competitive swing districts
Messaging discipline risk: progressive vs. moderate tension could fracture unified contrast message
filters: DCCC has strong incumbent protection protocols — will not primary vulnerable Dems
Jeffries has maintained unusually tight caucus discipline for a minority leader
overrides: If progressive base demands anti-war vote on Iran, leadership forced into a difficult position that alienates moderates OR base
Large fundraising haul (>$200M DCCC by Q3) removes resource constraint, enabling expansion of map

nrcc.name: NRCC (National Republican Congressional Committee)
nrcc.role: GOP incumbent protection and offense. Decides which seats to defend vs. abandon, and which marginal seats to contest.
nrcc.decisionStyle: Institutional. More risk-averse than Trump. Will pull resources from unwinnable races earlier than Trump White House wants.
biases: Likely to face internal conflict between protecting incumbents in Biden districts vs. enabling Trump endorsee primaries
Historical tendency to over-invest in offense (wave targets) when environment is favorable
filters: Cannot override Trump primary endorsements, even for unelectable candidates
FEC fundraising reporting creates public accountability on resource decisions
overrides: If environment deteriorates badly (generic ballot Dems +7+), NRCC shifts to pure incumbent defense mode — signals GOP concedes offense

voters_swing.name: Swing Voters / Soft Republicans in Suburban Districts
voters_swing.role: The marginal voters in the ~30 most competitive districts. These are primarily college-educated suburbanites, many of whom voted GOP in 2024 but have low economic satisfaction.
voters_swing.decisionStyle: Issue-driven (economy, healthcare, abortion), responsive to candidate quality, minimal party loyalty.
biases: Historically punish the president's party on the economy — tariff impact is directly legible to this cohort
Some rally-round-the-flag susceptibility if Iran war frames as existential defense
filters: Candidate quality matters more in swing districts than national environment
Local issues (housing, healthcare) can override national wave
overrides: If economic pain (rising prices, job losses) is personal and proximate by October, override national security framing

dem_base_enthusiasm.name: Democratic Base (Registered Dems, Lean-Dem independents)
dem_base_enthusiasm.role: Turnout driver. Midterm Democratic losses are frequently attributable to base enthusiasm collapse rather than swing voter defection. Activation of this group is necessary (not sufficient) for H1.
dem_base_enthusiasm.decisionStyle: Emotion-driven, responds to threat framing and visible injustice. Activated by abortion access, economic grievance, anti-war sentiment.
biases: Prone to enthusiasm collapse when they perceive party leadership as ineffective or complicit
More motivated by negative partisanship (against Trump) than positive agenda
filters: Abortion ballot measures in key states are a structural enthusiasm mechanism independent of candidate quality
Iran war anti-sentiment could activate young voter base specifically
overrides: If Democratic leadership takes a high-profile stand against tariffs or Iran war that resonates, enthusiasm spike possible

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — poll movement is data; pundit predictions are not. FEC filings, ballot certifications, special election results, and official approval ratings are the evidence inputs.
TAG EVERYTHING — separate polling data (POLL), fundraising reports (FUNDRAISING), special election outcomes (EVENT), and campaign rhetoric (RHETORIC) in the evidence log.
DON'T FRONT-RUN — set indicator tripwires and let September/October data confirm. Do not shift priors dramatically based on a single poll.

WATCH THE GENERIC BALLOT DIVERGENCE — the noted April 2026 divergence between Trump approval and generic ballot is the key epistemic uncertainty. Update both feeds monthly and track whether the gap narrows or widens.
WAR RALLY DECAY IS THE CRITICAL UNKNOWN — monitor monthly for whether rally effect dissipates. If it persists into September, all models tilt toward H3.
ECONOMIC PAIN TIMELINE — tariff impacts have a lag. Watch Q3 GDP and August/September CPI. If inflation spikes hit before October, H3/H4 probabilities collapse further.

generic_ballot_dem_advantage.label: Generic Congressional Ballot — Dem Advantage (pp)
generic_ballot_dem_advantage.note: Baseline is 2024 national House popular vote margin. Current value approx. April 2026 per stated context. Update from RCP average monthly.

trump_approval_overall.label: Trump Approval — Overall (%)
trump_approval_overall.note: Baseline is 2024 election day favorability (exit poll avg). Current per CNN April 2026. Update monthly from RCP average.

trump_approval_economy.label: Trump Approval — Economy (%)
trump_approval_economy.note: Baseline is approximate economy approval at 2025 inauguration. Career low per CNN April 2026. Update monthly.

trump_approval_net.label: Trump Net Approval (approve minus disapprove)
trump_approval_net.note: Baseline is approximately post-inauguration net approval. Current -19pp per stated context April 2026.

dccc_coh.label: DCCC Cash on Hand ($M)
dccc_coh.note: Populate from FEC Q1 2026 filing (due April 15, 2026). Update each quarterly filing.

nrcc_coh.label: NRCC Cash on Hand ($M)
nrcc_coh.note: Populate from FEC Q1 2026 filing (due April 15, 2026). Update each quarterly filing.

cook_tossup_lean_dem_seats.label: Cook Political Report — Toss Up + Lean Dem Seats (currently GOP-held)
cook_tossup_lean_dem_seats.note: Populate from Cook Political Report ratings. Update monthly. Key threshold: 20+ seats in these categories = structural advantage for Dems.

special_election_overperformance.label: Average Dem Special Election Overperformance vs. 2024 Baseline
special_election_overperformance.note: Calculate as mean Dem overperformance across all 2026 special elections vs. 2024 presidential result in same geography. Populate as specials occur.

watchpoints: FEC Q1 2026 quarterly fundraising reports due ~April 15. Populate dccc_coh and nrcc_coh immediately.
Any special House election called for a vacant seat — this is a tier-1 or tier-2 event.
Monthly RCP generic ballot average update — check first week of each month.
Monthly Trump approval tracking — Gallup, ABC/WaPo, CNN. Check mid-month.
Cook Political Report monthly ratings update — watch for seat movement in/out of Toss Up.
BLS CPI release each month — watch for tariff-linked inflation signal.
Iran/Hormuz conflict evolution — war rally decay or escalation directly affects structural environment.
State filing deadlines (most June-July 2026) — watch for Dem recruitment quality in top 30 targets and GOP incumbent retirement announcements.
September 2026 is the last major update window before the election. All tier-1 and tier-2 indicators should resolve by then.
Watch for Dem generic ballot divergence explanation: does the gap between Trump individual approval and generic ballot close (normalizes to historical pattern) or persist (structural GOP floor)?
searchQueries: DCCC NRCC fundraising FEC quarterly report 2026
Republican incumbent retirement 2026 House filing deadline
DCCC Red to Blue 2026 candidate recruitment
Iran war approval rally effect decay 2026
House generic ballot midterm historical model Abramowitz 2026

predictionScoring._docs: Snapshots auto-created by engine on each posterior update. Outcome recorded after AP calls House majority (est. November 3-4, 2026). Compute Brier scores at resolution.
